---
title: "Computational Musicology"
format: 
  dashboard:
    orientation: columns
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
---

# Weekly Assignments {width=50%}

## Course Assignments

::: {.card}
### Week 7: Computational Musicology and (Audio) Visualisation
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 8: Chroma Features
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 9: Timbre Features / Self-Similarity Matrices
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 10: Key and Chord Estimation
(not available right now, will be added later)
:::

::: {.card}
### Week 11: Novelty Functions and Tempograms
Analysis of Afraid to Feel by LF System

[Open page](week11.html)

:::

::: {.card}
### Week 12: Classification and clustering
Clustering my spotify 2025 wrapped 

[Open page](week12.html)

:::

# Portfolio {width=50%}

## Portfolio

### About me

I am Stan Pieter van Kuik, a student at the University of Amsterdam. This page show my portfolio for the course *Computational Musicology*.

### Course focus

In this portfolio, I explore music using computational methods such as audio visualisation, chroma analysis, timbre features, self-similarity matrices, key estimation, chord estimation, novelty functions, and tempograms.

### GitHub Repository

[View repository](https://github.com/stanpietervankuik/comp_music.git)
